risk_domain.name: Sequencer-Specific Privacy Risks
risk_domain.description: Privacy risks arising from centralized and shared L2 sequencers. RPC guard that detects sequencer-level threats: transaction content leakage, censorship, MEV extraction, and cross-rollup correlation. Extends l2_anonymity_set H2.

capabilities: full visibility of all transactions before batch inclusion (sequencer operator)
ability to reorder transactions within a batch for MEV extraction
cross-rollup correlation when operating as shared sequencer
selective censorship of specific addresses or transaction types
timing analysis of transaction submission patterns
limitations: cannot forge transactions or modify calldata
L1 forced inclusion provides eventual censorship resistance

H1_centralized_sequencer_risk.name: Centralized Sequencer Risk
H1_centralized_sequencer_risk.description: Transactions may be exposed to risks such as censorship or manipulation by a single sequencer entity.

signals.name: centralized_sequencer_entity
signals.description: Identify if a single entity operates the L2 sequencer without rotation or decentralization.

signals.name: no_encryption_mempool
signals.description: Detect if the L2 does not implement encrypted mempool or commit-reveal for transaction content.

signals.name: direct_transaction_submission
signals.description: Check if transactions are submitted directly to the sequencer endpoint without privacy-preserving measures like Tor/VPN.

signals.name: censorship_missing_inclusion
signals.description: Identify transactions that are not included within the expected time window despite being valid and with sufficient gas.

recommendations.description: Decentralize the sequencer role among multiple entities.
Encrypt the mempool and enable commit-reveal for transaction content.
Submit transactions via anonymous endpoints like Tor or a VPN.
Implement mechanisms to detect and mitigate censorship attempts proactively.

H2_sequencer_censorship.name: Sequencer Censorship
H2_sequencer_censorship.description: Transactions are selectively excluded from being processed within expected time windows, indicating potential censorship practices.

signals.name: tx_time_window
signals.description: Transaction not included in the blockchain within an expected time window, while having sufficient gas and valid nonce.

signals.name: batch_exclusion
signals.description: Same sender's transactions being excluded in multiple consecutive batches while others are included within the same batch.

signals.name: low_gas_comparison
signals.description: Transactions with lower gas price than a specific transaction being included, while higher gas transactions are not.

recommendations.description: Increase the transaction gas price to compete for inclusion.
Utilize priority fees or plugins that optimize for quick transactions.
Regularly check network status and transaction inclusion.
Work with the blockchain provider to whitelist your address.

H3_mev_extraction.name: MEV Extraction
H3_mev_extraction.description: Sequencers manipulate transaction order or content to extract unfair profits.

signals.name: transaction_order_mismatch
signals.description: Identifies cases where batch transaction order does not match user submission order or gas priority.

signals.name: adjacent_profitability
signals.description: Detects instances where a sequencer-controlled address profits from transactions adjacent to user's in the same batch.

signals.name: fair_ordering_recommendation
signals.description: Checks if users follow advice to prefer L2s that implement fair first-come, first-served (FCFS) or similar mechanisms.

recommendations.description: Use Layer 2 networks that enforce fair first-come, first-served (FCFS) transaction ordering.
Regularly check for transaction order mismatches using automated tools.
Utilize multiple Layer 2 providers to spread the risk.
Temporarily disable sequencer services if suspicious behavior is detected.

H4_shared_sequencer_linkage.name: Shared Sequencer Linkage
H4_shared_sequencer_linkage.description: Use of shared sequencers across multiple rollups can link user activities on different networks.

signals.name: shared_sequencer_linkage
signals.description: Identifies when users interact with rollups that share the same sequencer.

signals.name: correlated_timing_patterns
signals.description: Detects correlated timing patterns of transactions on different rollups that share a sequencer.

signals.name: bridging_transactions
signals.description: Analyzes transaction amounts to determine if they suggest use of the same shared sequencer for bridging.

recommendations.description: Disable shared sequencers to prevent linking activities across networks.
Monitor and log all transactions involving shared sequencers for anomaly detection.
Ensure each rollup uses a unique sequencer to avoid shared linkage.
Set up analytics tools to detect and alert on correlated timing patterns across rollups.

H5_pre_confirmation_privacy_leak.name: Pre-Confirmation Privacy Leak
H5_pre_confirmation_privacy_leak.description: Exposure of full transaction details before final confirmation increases the risk of deanonymization.

signals.name: pre_confirmation_details
signals.description: Presence of full transaction details in pre-confirmations before final L1 confirmation.

signals.name: long_pre_confirmation_gap
signals.description: Time difference between pre-confirmation and L1 batch posting is greater than 10 minutes.

signals.name: public_api_access
signals.description: Pre-confirmation data accessible via public API without authentication required.

recommendations.description: Delay the release of pre-confirmation details until after the first L1 confirmation.
Require authentication for accessing pre-confirmation data via public API.
Reduce the time between pre-confirmation and L1 batch posting to minimize exposure risk.

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring sequencer privacy risk detection across L2 networks

methodology.step_1: Survey sequencer architecture of top N L2 networks by TVL
methodology.step_2: Submit test transactions and measure inclusion timing, ordering fidelity, pre-confirmation exposure
methodology.step_3: Detect censorship patterns via controlled exclusion experiments
methodology.step_4: Measure cross-rollup correlation capability for shared sequencer setups
methodology.step_5: Score each L2 on sequencer privacy risk (centralization, MEV, censorship, linkage, preconf)

combined_benchmark.target: accurate risk classification of L2 sequencer privacy for top 20 L2 networks

adversary_levels.name: passive_observer
adversary_levels.description: Reads public pre-confirmations and sequencer data

adversary_levels.name: active_sequencer
adversary_levels.description: Sequencer operator extracting MEV and censoring

templates.risk_assessment: L2 transaction on {chain_name} triggers {heuristic_id} ({heuristic_name}): {signal_description}. Confidence: {confidence}. Sequencer risk: {risk_level}. Recommendation: {recommendation}.
templates.summary: Analyzed {n_txs} L2 transactions across {n_chains} chains. {n_risky} flagged for sequencer privacy risks ({pct_risky}%). Top risks: {top_risks}.
templates.skill_suggestion: To mitigate {heuristic_name}, I can {skill_action}. This reduces sequencer privacy exposure by ~{effectiveness_pct}%. {user_cost_note}
